input.topic: In the SDLC, how does Sprint differ from Epic & how do they relate to each other, when it comes to SDLC especially in a corporate environment
questions: What are the formal definitions of Epic vs Sprint in Agile SDLC?
How do Epics and Sprints relate hierarchically (Epic > Story > Sprint)?
How do corporate Agile frameworks (SAFe, LeSS, Scrum@Scale) structure Epic-to-Sprint flow?
What is the typical lifecycle of an Epic from inception to completion across Sprints?
How does Sprint planning incorporate Epic decomposition in enterprise environments?
What are common anti-patterns in managing the Epic-Sprint relationship at scale?
